key_points: Federalism is a system where power is shared between central and state governments
In federal systems there are two or more levels of government
The Constitution divides powers between these levels
The judiciary resolves disputes between governments
India is described as a Union of States
examples: India follows a federal system of government
USA is an example of a coming together federation
India is an example of a holding together federation
State governments manage subjects like police and agriculture
exam_tips: Remember the definition of federalism clearly
Know the difference between Union and State powers
Understand the role of the Constitution in dividing powers
